1.question: Who wrote the Gospel of Matthew, and what was his occupation before following Jesus?
To whom did Matthew chiefly write his gospel?
options: To the Jews, showing that Jesus is the promised Messiah

1.question: Why does Matthew open with Jesus’ genealogy from Abraham and David?
options: To show that Jesus is the promised heir, descended from David and Abraham
To list the kings of Egypt
To count the years of captivity

1.title: Three times fourteen
1.text: Matthew arranges the genealogy in three sets of fourteen generations: from Abraham to David, from David to the captivity in Babylon, and from the captivity to Christ.

1.template: ¶ Now the birth of Jesus {0} was on this wise: When as his mother Mary was espoused to Joseph, before they came together, she was found with child of the Holy Ghost.
thou shalt call his name {0}: for he shall save his people from their sins

2.title: The wise men and the star
2.text: Scripture does not say how many wise men there were, nor call them kings; the tradition of “three” comes from the three gifts. They came from the east following a star.

2.template: for we have seen his {0} in the east, and are come to worship him
And he sent them to {0}, and said, Go and search diligently for the young child; and when ye have found him, bring me word again, that I may come and worship him also.

2.question: What gifts did the wise men offer the child Jesus?

2.template: In Rama was there a voice heard, lamentation, and weeping, and great mourning, {0} weeping for her children, and would not be comforted, because they are not.
But when he heard that {0} did reign in Judæa in the room of his father Herod, he was afraid to go thither: notwithstanding, being warned of God in a dream, he turned aside into the parts of Galilee:

3.question: What did John the Baptist preach in the wilderness?
options: Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand
That the temple would be rebuilt
That the law of Moses was abolished

3.title: Clothed in camel’s hair
3.text: John’s clothing of camel’s hair and leather girdle, and his diet of locusts and wild honey, echo the prophet Elijah, who dressed in much the same way.

3.template: God is able of these stones to raise up children unto {0}
he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost, and with {0}

3.question: What happened when Jesus was baptized?
options: The heavens opened and the Spirit descended like a dove
John saw a pillar of fire

4.title: Forty days
4.text: The fast of forty days and forty nights echoes Moses’ forty days on the mount and Israel’s forty years in the wilderness.

4.question: How did Jesus answer the tempter who told him to make bread from stones?
options: Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God
That he was too hungry to answer
That he would do the miracle later
That the devil must worship him first

4.template: sheweth him all the {0} of the world, and the glory of them
he came and dwelt in {0}, which is upon the sea coast

4.question: What did Jesus promise Simon Peter and Andrew when he called them?
options: To make them fishers of men
A place of honor in the temple

4.question: What did Jesus do as he went about all Galilee?
options: Taught in the synagogues, preached the gospel, and healed sickness

5.question: According to the Beatitude, what shall the pure in heart receive?

5.template: Ye are the {0} of the earth

5.question: What did Jesus say about the law and the prophets?
options: He came not to destroy them, but to fulfil them
He came to abolish them forever
He came to hide them from the people

5.title: “Raca” and the council
5.text: “Raca” was an Aramaic term of contempt, roughly “worthless one.” Jesus teaches that even anger and insult toward a brother are accountable to God.

5.question: What did Jesus say to do before offering your gift at the altar, if your brother has aught against you?
options: First be reconciled to your brother
Wait for the priest to decide

5.question: What did Jesus teach about looking on a woman to lust after her?
options: That one has already committed adultery in the heart
That it is only a harmless thought
That one must pay a fine

5.template: neither by {0}; for it is the city of the great King

5.question: In answer to “an eye for an eye,” what did Jesus teach?
options: To resist not evil and turn the other cheek
To ignore the offense in silence

5.template: Love your {0}, bless them that curse you

6.question: How did Jesus teach to give alms?
options: In secret, without sounding a trumpet before you

6.template: Give us this day our daily {0}

6.title: “For thine is the kingdom”
6.text: The Lord’s Prayer closes with a doxology — “for thine is the kingdom, and the power, and the glory” — acknowledging that all belongs to God.

6.question: How did Jesus teach to fast?
options: In secret, anointing the head and washing the face
By announcing the fast in the streets

pairs.right: let not thy left hand know what thy right hand doeth
enter into thy closet and shut thy door
anoint thine head and wash thy face

6.title: God or mammon
6.text: “Mammon” is an Aramaic word for wealth, here personified as a rival master. Jesus says no one can serve two masters.

6.question: What example did Jesus give to teach not to worry?
options: The fowls of the air, which sow not nor reap, and God feeds them

6.template: But seek ye first the {0} of God, and his righteousness

7.question: What did Jesus teach about seeing others’ faults?
options: First cast the beam out of your own eye

7.question: What is the Golden Rule taught by Jesus?
options: Do to others what you would have them do to you
Collect what is owed to you
Only help those who help you

7.title: The strait gate
7.text: Jesus contrasts two ways: the broad road many follow to destruction, and the narrow one few find, which leads to life.

7.question: According to Jesus, how are false prophets known?
Who shall enter the kingdom of heaven, according to Jesus?
options: The one who does the Father’s will, not who merely says 'Lord, Lord'

7.template: which built his house upon the {0}

8.question: Why did Jesus marvel at the centurion?
options: For his great faith, greater than any found in Israel
For his rank in the army

8.question: Whom did Jesus heal of a fever when he came into Peter’s house?
What did Jesus answer the scribe who promised to follow him anywhere?
options: The foxes have holes, but the Son of man has nowhere to lay his head

8.title: Isaiah’s prophecy
8.text: Matthew sees in Jesus’ healings the fulfillment of Isaiah: 'Himself took our infirmities, and bare our sicknesses.'

8.template: there arose a great {0} in the sea

8.question: What made dangerous the road where Jesus met the two demoniacs?
options: They were so fierce that no man could pass that way

8.template: the whole herd of {0} ran violently down a steep place

9.question: What did Jesus say first to the paralytic, before healing him?
options: Thy faith hath made thee whole

9.template: he saw a man, named {0}, sitting at the receipt of custom
I will have mercy, and not {0}

9.title: New wine in new bottles
9.text: Jesus uses the new cloth and new wine to show that his gospel does not fit old forms: it needs new bottles to be preserved.

9.question: What did the woman who had an issue of blood for twelve years do?
options: She touched the hem of Jesus’ garment, believing she would be healed
She asked the disciples to pray

9.question: What did Jesus say about the girl everyone thought was dead?
options: She is not dead, but sleepeth

9.question: What did Jesus say to the two blind men before opening their eyes?
options: According to your faith be it unto you

9.question: What did Jesus say on seeing the multitudes weary, like sheep without a shepherd?
options: The harvest is great, but the labourers are few

10.title: The twelve apostles
10.text: Matthew lists the twelve by name — among them two pairs of brothers (Peter and Andrew, James and John) and Matthew the publican himself.

10.question: Which apostle is identified as the one who would betray Jesus?
What did Jesus command the apostles to preach?
options: The kingdom of heaven is at hand

10.question: What did Jesus tell the apostles NOT to take on their journey?
options: Gold, silver, brass, nor two coats

10.template: be ye therefore wise as {0}, and harmless as doves

10.question: According to Jesus, who shall be saved amid persecution?
options: He that endureth to the end
Whoever denies the faith to survive

10.question: Whom did Jesus say to fear, rather than those who kill the body?
options: Him who is able to destroy both soul and body

10.question: What did Jesus promise to whoever confesses him before men?
options: To confess him also before the Father in heaven

10.question: What did Jesus say is required of whoever would be worthy of him?
options: To love him above father, mother, and children, and take up the cross
To make a pilgrimage to Jerusalem

10.question: What did Jesus say about whoever receives his disciples?
options: Whoever receives them receives him and him who sent him
Will be exempt from the law
Will be given a sign from heaven

11.question: What did John the Baptist send his disciples to ask Jesus?
options: Whether he was the one who should come, or if they should look for another
When he would be freed from prison
Who would be greatest in the kingdom

11.question: How did Jesus describe John the Baptist to the multitudes?
options: More than a prophet; the greatest born of women
A reed shaken by the wind

11.title: The messenger
11.text: Jesus applies to John the prophecy of Malachi about the messenger sent ahead to prepare the way of the Lord.

11.template: this is {0}, which was for to come

11.question: To what did Jesus compare that generation, pleased neither with John nor with him?
options: To children in the market who neither dance nor mourn

11.question: Why did Jesus rebuke Chorazin and Bethsaida?
options: Because they did not repent, despite the mighty works done in them

11.question: What invitation did Jesus give to the weary and heavy laden?
options: Come unto me, and I will give you rest
Wait for the coming of Elias

12.template: Have ye not read what {0} did, when he was an hungred

12.question: How did Jesus declare himself in regard to the sabbath?
What example did Jesus give to show it is lawful to do good on the sabbath?
options: Rescuing a sheep fallen into a pit on the sabbath

12.title: The chosen servant
12.text: Matthew quotes Isaiah 42 to present Jesus as the chosen, beloved servant who will declare judgment to the Gentiles without striving or crying out.

12.template: Behold my {0}, whom I have chosen

12.question: How did the prophet describe the meekness of the chosen servant?
options: A bruised reed he will not break, nor quench the smoking flax
He will rule with a rod of iron
He will cry aloud in the streets

12.question: Of what did the Pharisees accuse Jesus when they saw him cast out devils?
options: Casting them out by Beelzebub, prince of the devils

12.question: What did Jesus say proved the kingdom of God had come?
options: Casting out devils by the Spirit of God

12.question: What sin did Jesus say would not be forgiven, neither in this world nor the next?
What did Jesus say about the words we speak?
options: For every idle word men shall give account in the day of judgment

12.question: What sign did Jesus give, comparing himself to the prophet Jonas?
options: Three days and nights in the heart of the earth, as Jonas in the whale
Fire from heaven on the temple

12.question: What did Jesus say happens when the unclean spirit returns to the empty, swept house?
options: It brings seven worse spirits, and the last state is worse than the first

12.question: Whom did Jesus call his mother and his brethren?
options: Those who do the will of his Father in heaven

13.question: In the parable of the sower, what happened to the seed that fell by the way side?
options: The fowls came and devoured it
It withered for lack of root

13.question: Why did Jesus say he spoke to the people in parables?
options: Because to the disciples it was given to know the mysteries of the kingdom, but not to them
To hide the truth from everyone
Because they were easier to memorize

13.question: What did Jesus say about the disciples’ eyes and ears?
options: Blessed, for they see and hear what prophets desired to see
That they were closed to the kingdom
That they would see Elias return

pairs.right: The wicked one catches away what was sown
Receives with joy, but has no root
Cares and riches choke the word

13.question: In the parable of the tares, what did the enemy do while men slept?

13.template: is like to a grain of {0} seed, which a man took

13.question: In the explanation of the parable of the tares, what does the field represent?
What did the man do when he found the treasure hid in the field?
options: Sold all that he had and bought that field

13.template: when he had found one {0} of great price

13.question: In the parable of the net, what will the angels do at the end of the world?
options: Sever the wicked from among the just
Release the fish back to the sea

13.question: To what did Jesus compare the scribe instructed unto the kingdom of heaven?
options: A householder who brings out of his treasure things new and old

13.title: The brethren of Jesus
13.text: The people of Nazareth name Jesus’ brethren — James, Joses, Simon, and Judas — and mention his sisters too, showing him as part of a family they knew.

13.question: Why did Jesus not do many mighty works in his own country?

14.question: What did the daughter of Herodias ask of Herod, instructed by her mother?
options: The head of John the Baptist on a charger

14.question: What did Jesus do when he heard of John the Baptist’s death?
options: He withdrew by ship into a desert place
He went up to the temple

14.question: With how much did Jesus feed about five thousand men?
options: Seven loaves and a few fishes

14.question: What did Jesus do in the fourth watch of the night, going to the disciples?
options: He calmed the storm with a word
He sent an angel to the ship

14.title: Twelve baskets
14.text: After five thousand men ate and were filled, twelve baskets full of fragments remained — more than there had been at the start.

14.question: What happened to Peter as he walked on the water to go to Jesus?
options: He feared the wind and began to sink
He refused to leave the ship
He fell and was rescued by John

14.question: What did those in the ship say after the wind ceased?
options: Of a truth thou art the Son of God

15.question: By what did Jesus say the Pharisees made the commandment of God of none effect?

15.title: “It is a gift”
15.text: Some declared their goods “a gift” (corban) to escape the duty of supporting father and mother — using a religious tradition to dodge a commandment.

15.template: but their {0} is far from me

15.question: How did Jesus describe the Pharisees who were offended at his teaching?
options: Blind leaders of the blind, who shall fall into the ditch

15.question: According to Jesus, what truly defiles a man?
options: What comes out of the mouth, for it proceeds from the heart

15.question: What did the disciples ask Jesus concerning the Canaanite woman?
options: To send her away, for she cried after them
To take her to the temple

15.question: What did the Canaanite woman answer when Jesus spoke of the children’s bread and the dogs?
options: That even the dogs eat the crumbs from their masters’ table
That she gave up her request
That she was a daughter of Abraham
That she would pay for the miracle

15.question: Why did Jesus have compassion on the multitude before feeding the four thousand?
options: Because they had been with him three days with nothing to eat

15.question: How many baskets of fragments were left after feeding the four thousand?

16.question: What did Jesus answer the Pharisees and Sadducees who sought a sign from heaven?
options: You can discern the sky, but not the signs of the times

16.question: Why did the disciples think Jesus spoke of leaven because they had brought no bread?
options: They did not understand he meant the doctrine of the Pharisees

16.question: What did Jesus ask the disciples at Caesarea Philippi?
options: Whom men said the Son of man was

16.template: Thou art the {0}, the Son of the living God

16.title: Peter and the rock
16.text: The name Peter means “rock.” Jesus plays on this when he says, “thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church.”

16.question: What did Jesus say to Peter when he rebuked him for speaking of his death?
What did Jesus say about whoever would save his own life?
options: Whoever would save it shall lose it; whoever loses it for his sake shall find it
That he would gain the world
That he would have honor among men

17.question: Who appeared talking with Jesus when he was transfigured on the mount?
What did the disciples do when they heard the voice out of the cloud?
options: They fell on their face, sore afraid

17.question: To whom was Jesus referring when he said that “Elias is come already”?

17.template: If ye have faith as a grain of {0} seed

17.question: How did Jesus tell Peter to pay the temple tribute?
options: By taking a coin from the mouth of the first fish he caught

17.title: The coin in the fish
17.text: Jesus tells Peter he will find in a fish’s mouth a piece of money — a coin worth exactly the tribute for two people, enough for both him and Peter.

18.question: According to Jesus, who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven?
options: Whoever humbles himself as a little child

18.template: that a {0} were hanged about his neck

18.question: What did Jesus say about that which causes you to sin (hand, foot, or eye)?
options: It is better to enter life without it than to be cast into the fire
That it is cured by fasting

18.question: In the parable of the lost sheep, what does the shepherd of the hundred sheep do?
options: Leaves the ninety and nine and seeks the one that strayed
Waits for the sheep to return

18.question: What did Jesus promise about two or three gathered in his name?
options: There am I in the midst of them
They will be exempt from the law
They will see a sign from heaven

18.question: How many times did Jesus say we should forgive our brother?
In the parable, how much did the servant whom the king forgave owe him?
What did the forgiven servant do when he found a fellowservant who owed him a hundred pence?
options: He seized him and cast him into prison until he should pay
He paid the debt for him

18.title: Ten thousand talents
18.text: The forgiven debt — ten thousand talents — was an unpayable fortune, while the hundred pence the servant demanded was a trifle: the contrast measures God’s forgiveness.

18.question: What did the lord do when he learned the servant had no compassion on his fellowservant?
options: Delivered him to the tormentors until he should pay all

19.question: What did Jesus teach about marriage?
options: What God hath joined together, let not man put asunder
A man may put away his wife for any cause
Marriage does not matter in the kingdom
Only Moses could join a couple

19.question: Why did Moses allow a writing of divorcement, according to Jesus?
options: Because of the hardness of their hearts
Because God had so commanded from the beginning

19.question: What did Jesus say as he blessed the little children?
options: Suffer the little children to come unto me, for of such is the kingdom of heaven
That they should wait until grown
That the disciples teach them first

19.question: What did Jesus tell the rich young man who wished to be perfect?
options: Sell what thou hast, give to the poor, and follow me

19.template: for a camel to go through the eye of a {0}

19.title: The eye of a needle
19.text: The image of the camel and the needle — the largest local animal and the smallest known opening — was a deliberate exaggeration to show how hard it is to trust in riches instead of God.

19.question: What did Jesus promise those who had followed him?
options: To sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel
A place in the government of Rome

20.question: To what did Jesus compare the kingdom of heaven in this parable?
options: A householder who hires labourers for his vineyard

20.question: How much did each labourer receive, whether hired first or last?

20.title: The denarius
20.text: A penny (denarius) was a full day’s wage. That is why paying the same to those who worked only an hour sounded scandalous — which is exactly the point of the parable about grace.

20.template: So the last shall be first, and the first {0}

20.question: What did the mother of Zebedee’s sons ask of Jesus?
options: That her two sons might sit on his right and left hand in the kingdom
That he teach them to pray

20.template: but to minister, and to give his life a {0} for many

20.question: What did the two blind men near Jericho ask of Jesus?
options: That their eyes might be opened

21.question: How did the prophet say the King would come to the daughter of Sion?
options: Meek, and sitting upon an ass and a colt
At the head of an army

21.title: What "Hosanna" means
21.text: "Hosanna" comes from Hebrew and means "save us now." The crowd greeted Jesus with words from Psalm 118, hailing him as the promised King.

21.question: As he cleansed the temple, what did Jesus say they had made it?
Who cried "Hosanna to the Son of David" in the temple, displeasing the priests?
What happened to the fig tree Jesus found with only leaves?
How did Jesus respond to those who questioned his authority?
options: He asked them whence the baptism of John was

21.question: In the parable of the two sons, which one did the will of his father?
options: The one who said "I will not," but afterward repented and went
The one who said "I go, sir," and went

21.question: In the parable, what did the husbandmen do to the servants the owner sent?
options: They beat one, killed another, and stoned another
They received them with a feast

21.question: What did the husbandmen do when the owner sent his own son?
options: They killed him to seize the inheritance

21.question: To what did Jesus liken himself by quoting "the stone which the builders rejected"?
options: The head of the corner (chief cornerstone)

22.question: In the parable of the wedding, how did the first guests respond?
options: They made light of it and went to their own affairs

22.question: Whom did the servants gather to furnish the wedding?
options: All they found, both bad and good

22.template: For many are called, but few are {0}
Render therefore unto {0} the things which are Cæsar’s

22.title: The tribute coin
22.text: The tribute penny bore the image and title of the Roman emperor. By asking for the coin, Jesus showed that those questioning him already used Cæsar’s money.

22.question: What case did the Sadducees present to deny the resurrection?
options: Seven brothers who, one after another, had the same wife
A man who lived a thousand years

22.question: How did Jesus prove the resurrection against the Sadducees?
options: God is the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob — of the living, not the dead
By citing the law of divorce
By the number of the righteous
By the parable of the vineyard

22.question: According to Jesus, which is the first and great commandment?
options: To love God with all thy heart, soul, and mind

23.question: What did Jesus criticize in the scribes and Pharisees who sat in Moses’ seat?
options: They do not know the law
They do not attend the synagogue

23.question: What greeting did the scribes and Pharisees love to be called by men?

23.template: and he that shall humble himself shall be {0}

23.question: In the woe about oaths, what did Jesus say was greater?
options: The temple, which sanctifies the gold
The gold, which sanctifies the temple
The altar, which sanctifies the temple
The gift, which sanctifies the altar

23.question: They tithed mint, anise, and cummin, but omitted the weightier matters of the law — which?
To what did Jesus compare the hypocritical scribes and Pharisees?
options: Whited sepulchres, fair outside and full of bones within

23.title: Whited sepulchres
23.text: Before Passover, tombs were whitewashed so pilgrims would not touch them by mistake and become ceremonially unclean. Jesus uses that familiar image: fair without, unclean within.

23.question: Of what did Jesus say the Pharisees were witnesses against themselves?
options: That they were children of them who killed the prophets
That they kept the law well

23.template: even as a {0} gathereth her chickens under her wings

24.question: What was Jesus’ first warning about the last days?
options: That no man deceive them, for many would come in his name

24.question: What did Jesus say about wars and rumours of wars?
options: Not to be troubled, for the end was not yet
That it was the end of the world

24.title: The temple destroyed
24.text: Jesus said not one stone of the temple would be left upon another. In A.D. 70, about forty years later, the Romans destroyed Jerusalem and the temple, literally fulfilling the prophecy.

24.template: But he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be {0}

24.question: For whose sake would those days of great tribulation be shortened?
To what did Jesus compare the coming of the Son of man?
options: The lightning that comes from the east and shines to the west

24.template: but my words shall not pass {0}

24.question: What image did Jesus use for the separation at his coming?
options: Two in the field: one taken, the other left

24.question: What counsel did Jesus give because the Son of man comes at an unexpected hour?
What distinguishes the faithful servant from the evil servant in the closing parable?
options: The faithful tends the household; the evil beats his fellows and gets drunk

25.title: How much was a talent worth?
25.text: A “talent” was a unit of weight; in silver it equalled many years of a laborer’s wages. The servant who buried “one” talent still hid a small fortune.

25.question: How many of the ten virgins were wise?
What lesson did Jesus draw from the ten virgins?
options: Watch, for ye know neither the day nor the hour

25.question: How many talents did the third servant receive?
What did the lord say to the servants who doubled their talents?
options: “Well done, thou good and faithful servant”

25.question: What did the slothful servant do with his talent?
options: He hid it in the earth
He gave it to the exchangers
He gave it to the poor

25.question: Serving “one of the least of these,” the King said, is as if done to whom?
Where do the righteous go, according to verse 46?

26.title: Gethsemane: the “oil press”
26.text: The name Gethsemane means “oil press.” It was an olive garden on the Mount of Olives where olives were crushed for oil—a fitting image of what Jesus suffered there.

26.question: In whose palace did the leaders gather to plot against Jesus?
In whose house was Jesus anointed at Bethany?
Why did Jesus say the woman poured the ointment on him?
options: To prepare him for his burial

26.template: they covenanted with him for {0} pieces of silver

26.question: How did Jesus indicate at the table who would betray him?
options: “He that dippeth his hand with me in the dish”
“He that sitteth on my right hand”

26.question: At the Last Supper, what did Jesus say the bread represented?
How many times did Jesus say Peter would deny him?
To what place did Jesus go to pray in agony?
How many times did Jesus pray in Gethsemane?
What sign did Judas arrange to identify Jesus?
What did the false witnesses accuse Jesus of?
options: Of saying he could destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days

26.question: What did the council decide Jesus was?
What did Peter do when accused of being with Jesus?
What happened immediately after Peter’s third denial?

27.title: Golgotha, the “place of a skull”
27.text: “Golgotha” is Aramaic for “place of a skull” (Calvary in Latin). It was a place of execution outside the walls of Jerusalem.

27.question: What did Judas do after seeing Jesus condemned?
What was bought with the thirty pieces of silver?
How did Jesus respond to the many accusations before Pilate?
What did Pilate do to declare himself innocent of Jesus’ blood?
options: He washed his hands before the multitude

27.question: Who was compelled to carry Jesus’ cross?
Who were crucified with Jesus, one on each side?
What did Jesus cry with a loud voice about the ninth hour?
options: “My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?”
“Into thy hands I commend my spirit”

27.question: What did the centurion say when he saw the earthquake?
options: “Truly this was the Son of God”

27.question: Who asked Pilate for Jesus’ body and buried it?
What did the chief priests ask Pilate to do after the burial?
options: Set a guard and seal the tomb

28.title: The “Great Commission”
28.text: Verses 19–20 are called the “Great Commission”: Jesus’ charge to take the gospel to all nations. It is the only place in Matthew that names the Father, the Son, and the Holy Ghost together.

28.question: Who rolled back the stone from the door of the tomb?

28.template: for he is {0}, as he said

28.question: What story were the soldiers paid to tell?
options: That the disciples stole the body by night
That the body turned to dust
That an angel took the body

28.question: In whose name did Jesus command to baptize all nations?
options: The Father, the Son, and the Holy Ghost
